rulings.Case Title: C.I. ENERGIA SOLAR S.A., INC., VS. BAGATELOS GLASS SYSTEMS, INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To File First Amended Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 25, 2019, Line 1, CROSS COMPLAINANT BAGATELOS GLASS SYSTEMS, INC., THE HANOVER INSURANCE COMPANY, Motion For Leave To File First Amended Cross-Complaint. Cross-Complainant Bagatelos Glass Systems, Inc.'s motion for leave to file a first amended cross-complaint is granted on the condition that Bagatelos plead its new fraud theory as a separate cause of action so as to avoid delaying the resolution of C.I. Energia Solar S.A.'s pending motion for summary adjudication set for hearing on April 5, 2019. The policy favoring liberal amendment of pleadings supports the motion. Cross-Defendant C.I. Energia Solar S.A. has not shown that it would be prejudiced by allowing the amendment. Any challenges to whether ESWindows, LLC is subject to personal jurisdiction or whether the amended cross-complaint states a cause of action may be raised separately. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Bagatelos is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For 1) Ccp 2019.030 Protective Order To Restrict Frequency Or Extent Of Use Of Depositions By Defendants 2) For Monetary Santions Per Ccp2019.030(C) And 3) For Ccp 2023.030 (A) Monetary Sanctions For Misuse Of Discovery Process, In The Alternative
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 25, 2019, Line 1, PLAINTIFF DEBORAH CHATMAN'S Amended Motion For 1) Ccp 2019.030 Protective Order To Restrict Frequency Or Extent Of Use Of Depositions By Defendants 2) For Monetary Santions Per Ccp2019.030(C) And 3) For Ccp 2023.030 (A) Monetary Sanctions For Misuse Of Discovery Process, In The Alternative. Pro Tem Judge Douglas Robbins, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties ordered to appear. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to drobbins@woodrobbins.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER WALKER ET AL VS. HAND JOB, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Admissions (Set Two And Set Three) And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 25, 2019, Line 3, PLAINTIFF JENNIFER WALKER, ANDREA WALKER Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Admissions (Set Two And Set Three) And Request For Sanctions. Hearing required. Counsel for Defendants is directed to bring proof of the revival of the status of the second Hand Job, LLC to the hearing on March 25, 2019. While suspended corporate capacity precludes the corporation from prosecuting or defending any action (Rev. & Tax. Code §23301; Timberline, Inc. v. Jaisinghani (1997) 54 Cal.App.4th 1361, 1365), "[s]ubsequent corporate reviver retroactively validates actions in the course of litigation such as . . . making and opposing motions and engaging in discovery." (Center for Self-Improvement & Community Development v. Lennar Corp. (2009) 173 Cal.App.4th 1543, 1553 [citing Diverco Constructors, Inc. v. Wilstein (1970) 4 Cal.App.3d 6, 12].) The Court will consider the motion on the merits only if it determines that the second Hand Job, LLC has been revived from its suspended status. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: EDGARD VELARDE VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Punitive Damages From Plaintiffs Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 25, 2019, Line 4, DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. Motion To Strike Punitive Damages From Plaintiffs Amended Complaint. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been sent to the parties by email). As a threshold matter, a motion to strike must be filed within the time allowed to respond to a pleading, i.e., within 30 days of service. (Code Civ. Proc. § 435(b)(1).) Here, the first amended complaint was filed on August 22, 2018 and amended on February 14, 2019. While Defendant has not explained why it did not file its motion earlier, there is no prejudice to Plaintiff, as Defendant has not yet filed a responsive pleading, and Plaintiff did not seek entry of default. Plaintiff did not serve its opposition on Defendants, wrongly named one of the moving defendants, and did not file an opposition to the motion filed by Defendant Uber Technologies, Inc. Because the motions are identical and both defendants received the opposition and filed replies, however, there is no prejudice, and the Court will address the motions on their merits. Plaintiff is admonished that it must serve copies of its papers and deliver courtesy copies to the Court, as required by the Code and by the Court's Local Rules. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: BRYANT FU VS. MEI LING FANG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 25, 2019, Line 7, PLAINTIFF BRYANT FU DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant Mora's demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to allege facts showing how Mr. Yan harmed him as opposed to his father. The real party in interest must prosecute an action per CCP 367. In addition, the claims are time-barred. The statute of limitations for fraud is 3 years under CCP 338(d). Plaintiff is complaining about transfers that occurred years ago and that have been the subject of prior suits between Tony Fu (plaintiff's father) and Mr. Yan. (See Complaint, 34, 45 and Complaint in CGC 15-549575, 44.) Tony Fu verified his complaint on December 21, 2015 in CGC 15-549575 and plaintiff filed this untimely action more than 3 years later on December 27, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Defendant Mora is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
